behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: The real failure boundary is the wiring between summary-write logic and persisted per-dimension findings (including round scoping). A unit test of summary composition alone can pass while storage query/filter bugs still allow invalid PASS saves.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real local test DB schema. Seed round R with mixed findings including at least one FAIL. Attempt to persist a PASS summary for round R through the real repository/service path. Assert the write is rejected and returns a domain error explaining blocking FAIL findings. Also verify PASS is allowed for a different round with all PASS findings to confirm correct round scoping.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: The bug came from trusting summary text over stored findings, which is a cross-module data-source selection problem. Pure unit mocks can hide incorrect source precedence.
behaviors.test_description: Using local persistence, seed findings for a round containing a FAIL verdict, then store a conflicting manual summary text claiming all PASS. Invoke stop-gate verdict computation through the real gate service. Assert final verdict is not PASS and that decision rationale references stored findings rather than summary text fields.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: This behavior spans validation, write path, and audit logging. A unit test can miss failures where warning records are not persisted or not linked to the override event.
behaviors.test_description: Through the real summary submission API/service with local DB: submit manual override text without override flag and assert rejection. Then submit with override flag and assert acceptance plus creation of an audit warning record containing actor, timestamp, round id, and override reason metadata.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the review loop cannot finalize PASS unless a fresh review-agent round is executed on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: The core risk is stale-state orchestration (diff/version tracking + round freshness check), not LLM output quality. A realistic integration harness is needed to verify state transitions across phases and rounds.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate workflow state in local DB: complete a review round on diff hash D1, apply Phase 4 fixes producing D2, then attempt finalize without running a new round. Assert finalize is blocked with stale-review reason. Next run a new review round recorded against D2 (stubbed agent execution marker is sufficient), then assert finalize can proceed only if stored findings allow PASS.

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings, the flow emits an escalation outcome instead of PASS.
behaviors.justification: This is a policy/state-machine check across persisted round counters and unresolved MUST-FIX findings. Mock-only tests can miss off-by-one or persistence query defects that incorrectly permit PASS.
behaviors.test_description: Seed scenario with max rounds reached and at least one unresolved MUST-FIX finding in storage. Invoke final decision path through the real orchestration service. Assert outcome is ESCALATION (or equivalent) and explicitly not PASS. Add boundary cases (max-1 rounds, resolved MUST-FIX) to ensure escalation triggers only at true exhaustion with unresolved required fixes.
